title: Release Reconciliation, Incident Response, and Production Defense · Language Lens: Feed Go End To End Proof
summary: The team has many passing checks but cannot bind source to artifact, explain feed loss, prove publisher respect, restore service, or identify residual risks. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go end to end proof increment in defensible production Go feed aggregator. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go End To End Proof · Theory Feed Go End To End Proof
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5017 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend source-to-artifact provenance, unfamiliar end-to-end behavior, incidents, recovery, and residual risk; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Competency probe: Demonstrate unfamiliar RSS and Atom sources from authorized discovery through bounded fetch, normalization, transaction, scheduling, API, accessible reader, and cleanup.

For Feed aggregation and Go Defend the Feed Go End To End Proof model · Theory Feed Go End To End Proof, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Replaying workshop fixtures is sufficient final mastery evidence.
